description: Establish and document a green baseline for both target apps before any UI changes. Install monorepo dependencies and record the current typecheck/lint/test results for apps/mobile-staff and apps/web so later features can be judged against a known starting point.
steps: From the repo root (/projects/sandbox/revathysupermarket) run `npm install`. The npm registry is reachable. Do not change React from 19.1.0 (pinned via root overrides).
Staff baseline (apps/mobile-staff): run `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npm run lint`. Capture pass/fail and any pre-existing errors verbatim.
Web baseline (apps/web): run `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test`. Capture pass/fail and pre-existing errors verbatim.

Optionally attempt `npm run build:web` from repo root with placeholder env: UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL=https://example.com UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN=dummy DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/db . If it fails, note whether the failure is environmental (missing services/env) vs a code error.

Record all results in this feature's `findings` field: which commands pass, which fail, and any pre-existing errors that are NOT in scope to fix. This becomes the reference baseline for FEAT-002..005.
acceptance_criteria: `npm install` completes at the repo root without fatal errors.
tsc, lint (and web `npm test`) have been run for both apps and their exact pass/fail status is recorded in findings.
Any pre-existing (baseline) failures are documented so they are not mistaken for regressions introduced by later features.
verification: cd apps/mobile-staff && npx tsc --noEmit ; npm run lint
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit ; npm run lint ; npm test

findings.env: Node v22.22.3, npm 11.4.2. npm registry reachable. Network install OK.
findings.npm_install: PASS (exit 0). Added 1952 packages. Only deprecation warnings + npm audit advisories (33 vulns: 2 low, 30 moderate, 1 high) - informational, not fatal. React stays pinned at 19.1.0.
findings.staff_tsc: FAIL (exit 2) - 1 PRE-EXISTING error. src/services/notifications.ts(7,35): TS2322 - setNotificationHandler return object is missing NotificationBehavior props 'shouldShowBanner' and 'shouldShowList' (expo-notifications type API change). This is a baseline error unrelated to UI/animation work; NOT introduced by later features. Treat as pre-existing unless a later feature touches notifications.ts.
findings.staff_lint: FAIL (config, PRE-EXISTING) - `npm run lint` (eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx) errors: 'ESLint couldn't find an eslint.config.(js|mjs|cjs) file.' ESLint 9.39.4 (resolved from hoisted root node_modules) requires flat config. apps/mobile-staff has NO eslint config file at all and does NOT list eslint in its devDependencies. So staff lint has never been runnable in this repo state. This is a pre-existing setup gap, NOT a regression. Staff verification for later features should rely on `npx tsc --noEmit` (baseline = the single notifications.ts error above). Do not treat missing-config lint failure as a regression.
findings.web_prisma_generate_required: IMPORTANT: web tsc/build depend on a generated Prisma client. Fresh checkout after npm install has NO generated client, so `npx tsc --noEmit` reports 329 errors (Prisma namespace missing InputJsonValue/JsonNull, untyped query results). After running `npx prisma generate` (which `npm run build` does automatically via 'prisma generate && next build'), tsc is clean. Always run `npx prisma generate` in apps/web before typechecking.
findings.web_tsc: PASS (0 errors) AFTER `npx prisma generate`. Clean baseline.
findings.web_lint: PASS (0 errors, 3 warnings). Pre-existing warnings (NOT errors, not gating): components/dashboard/customer-orders-client.tsx:235 @next/next/no-img-element; components/product-grid.tsx:212 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps (missing 'allItems'); lib/hooks/use-order-tracking-socket.ts:290 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps (missing 'fallbackToSSE').
findings.web_test: FAIL overall (exit 1): 63 tests, 61 pass, 2 fail. Both failures are PRE-EXISTING (no source changed in this feature): (1) not ok 47 'checkoutErrorResponse returns 400 for DELIVERY_OUT_OF_RANGE' at tests/order-checkout.test.ts:58 - assert.ok(result.error.includes("delivery-fee range")) is falsy. (2) not ok 54 'root elements allow native scrolling' at tests/scroll-preservation.test.ts - globals.css body contains 'touch-action: pan-x pan-y;' which the test asserts should NOT match /touch-action\s*:/. NOTE for FEAT-002..005: test #54 inspects app/globals.css; dark-theme/CSS edits to globals.css could affect it - re-run `npm test` after globals.css changes and compare against this 2-fail baseline. These 2 failures existed before any UI work and must not be counted as regressions.
findings.web_build: PASS (exit 0, optional). `npm run build:web` from repo root with placeholder env (UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL=https://example.com UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN=dummy DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@localhost:5432/db) => 2 tasks successful. next build + prisma generate completed. Without the UPSTASH placeholder env, prebuild (scripts/verify-production-env.mjs) fails under NODE_ENV=production; that is an environmental gate, not a code error.
findings.no_source_changes: This feature changed no application source. Only regenerated apps/web/tsconfig.tsbuildinfo (reverted) and node_modules (.prisma client, untracked). Baseline documentation only.
findings.baseline_gates_for_later_features: Staff: `npx tsc --noEmit` baseline = exactly 1 error in src/services/notifications.ts (lint not runnable - no config). Web: `npx prisma generate` then `npx tsc --noEmit` = 0 errors; `npm run lint` = 0 errors/3 warnings; `npm test` = 61 pass / 2 fail (#47 order-checkout, #54 scroll-preservation).
